evidence: FACT 1 (emit claim is TRUE): `interrupt.stt` has a defined packet type (packets.ts:238 `InterruptSttPacket { kind: "interrupt.stt" }`) and a Deepgram listener (voice-stt-deepgram/src/index.ts:185 `bus.on("interrupt.stt", () => { this.streamStartTime = Date.now(); this.resetTurnTranscriptState(); })`), but NO emitter exists anywhere in packages/. There is no `make.interruptStt` factory in packet-factories.ts (only `interruptTts` at :169 and `interruptLlm` at :173), and grep across all of packages/ for `interruptStt`/`interrupt.stt` push sites returns zero runtime emitters. handleInterruptDetected (voice-agent-session.ts:955-957) pushes only `make.interruptTts` and `make.interruptLlm` on Route.Critical. So `interrupt.stt` is a defined-but-never-emitted dead protocol path; the Google STT plugin does not even listen for it (voice-stt-google/src/index.ts has no `interrupt.stt` handler). FACT 2 (the leak claim is essentially FALSE): transcript state is rigorously keyed by contextId â€” `transcriptStateByContextId = new Map<string, ProviderTranscriptState>()` (:88), and every read/write/emit is context-scoped: `transcriptState(providerContextId)` (:225), `pushInterim(transcript, providerContextId)` (:273), `pushFinal(...contextId)` (:368), guarded by `finalizedContextIds.has(providerContextId)` (:223). `resetTurnTranscriptState()` only does `resetPendingTranscript(this.currentContextId)` â†’ `transcriptStateByContextId.delete(contextId)` (:447-452). A new user turn after barge-in arrives with a NEW client-supplied contextId (voice-server-websocket/src/index.ts:650 `binaryAudio.contextId`, :680 `message.contextId ?? contextId()`), which fires `turn.change` (only when `nextContextId !== currentContextId`, lines 651/681/694) and the Deepgram `turn.change` handler (:178-182) advances `currentContextId`. The stale interim from the interrupted context lives under the OLD contextId key and `transcriptState(newContextId)` returns a FRESH empty entry â€” so there is no cross-context stale-transcript leak into the post-barge-in turn.
real_fix: No stale-transcript-leak fix is needed because transcript state is per-contextId keyed and the next turn uses a new contextId. The genuine residual issues are: (1) `interrupt.stt` is dead protocol surface â€” a defined packet + a Deepgram listener with no emitter. Either delete the dead path (remove the `bus.on("interrupt.stt")` handler at voice-stt-deepgram/src/index.ts:185-188 and the `InterruptSttPacket` type at packets.ts:238-240) OR, if you want defense-in-depth for the narrow case where a client reuses the SAME contextId across a barge-in (in which `turn.change` does not fire because nextContextId===currentContextId, guarded at voice-server-websocket lines 651/681/694), wire it: add `interruptStt(contextId, timestampMs)` to packet-factories.ts and push `make.interruptStt(pkt.contextId, pkt.timestampMs)` on Route.Critical in handleInterruptDetected (voice-agent-session.ts after :957). The kimi-claimed harm (stale transcript leaking into the post-barge-in turn) does not occur on the normal new-contextId path; the wiring is only protocol-hygiene / a guard for a non-default client contract, hence low severity.
